     253              : 
     254              : /*****************************************************************************
     255              :  *
     256              :  *         Query optimizer entry point
     257              :  *
     258              :  * To support loadable plugins that monitor or modify planner behavior,
     259              :  * we provide a hook variable that lets a plugin get control before and
     260              :  * after the standard planning process.  The plugin would normally call
     261              :  * standard_planner().
     262              :  *
     263              :  * Note to plugin authors: standard_planner() scribbles on its Query input,
     264              :  * so you'd better copy that data structure if you want to plan more than once.
     265              :  *
     266              :  *****************************************************************************/
     267              : PlannedStmt *
     268          439 : planner(Query *parse, const char *query_string, int cursorOptions,
     269              :                 ParamListInfo boundParams)
     270              : {
     271              :         PlannedStmt *result;
     272              : 
     273          439 :         if (planner_hook)
     274            0 :                 result = (*planner_hook) (parse, query_string, cursorOptions, boundParams);
     275              :         else
     276          439 :                 result = standard_planner(parse, query_string, cursorOptions, boundParams);
     277          439 :         return result;
     278              : }
     279              : 
     280              : PlannedStmt *
     281          439 : standard_planner(Query *parse, const char *query_string, int cursorOptions,
     282              :                                  ParamListInfo boundParams)
     283              : {
     284              :         PlannedStmt *result;
     285              :         PlannerGlobal *glob;
     286              :         double          tuple_fraction;
     287              :         PlannerInfo *root;
     288              :         RelOptInfo *final_rel;

     292              :                            *lr;
     293              : 
     294              :         /*
     295              :          * Set up global state for this planner invocation.  This data is needed
     296              :          * across all levels of sub-Query that might exist in the given command,
     297              :          * so we keep it in a separate struct that's linked to by each per-Query
     298              :          * PlannerInfo.
     299              :          */
     300          439 :         glob = makeNode(PlannerGlobal);
     301              : 
     302          439 :         glob->boundParams = boundParams;
     303          439 :         glob->subplans = NIL;
     304          439 :         glob->subroots = NIL;
     305          439 :         glob->rewindPlanIDs = NULL;
     306          439 :         glob->finalrtable = NIL;
     307          439 :         glob->finalrowmarks = NIL;
     308          439 :         glob->resultRelations = NIL;
     309          439 :         glob->rootResultRelations = NIL;
     310          439 :         glob->appendRelations = NIL;
     311          439 :         glob->relationOids = NIL;
     312          439 :         glob->invalItems = NIL;
     313          439 :         glob->paramExecTypes = NIL;
     314          439 :         glob->lastPHId = 0;
     315          439 :         glob->lastRowMarkId = 0;
     316          439 :         glob->lastPlanNodeId = 0;
     317          439 :         glob->transientPlan = false;
     318          439 :         glob->dependsOnRole = false;
     319              : 
     320              :         /*
     321              :          * Assess whether it's feasible to use parallel mode for this query. We
     322              :          * can't do this in a standalone backend, or if the command will try to
     323              :          * modify any data, or if this is a cursor operation, or if GUCs are set
     324              :          * to values that don't permit parallelism, or if parallel-unsafe
     325              :          * functions are present in the query tree.
     326              :          *
     327              :          * (Note that we do allow CREATE TABLE AS, SELECT INTO, and CREATE
     328              :          * MATERIALIZED VIEW to use parallel plans, but this is safe only because
     329              :          * the command is writing into a completely new table which workers won't
     330              :          * be able to see.  If the workers could see the table, the fact that
     331              :          * group locking would cause them to ignore the leader's heavyweight
     332              :          * GIN page locks would make this unsafe.  We'll have to fix that somehow
     333              :          * if we want to allow parallel inserts in general; updates and deletes
     334              :          * have additional problems especially around combo CIDs.)
     335              :          *
     336              :          * For now, we don't try to use parallel mode if we're running inside a
     337              :          * parallel worker.  We might eventually be able to relax this
     338              :          * restriction, but for now it seems best not to have parallel workers
     339              :          * trying to create their own parallel workers.
     340              :          */
     341          439 :         if ((cursorOptions & CURSOR_OPT_PARALLEL_OK) != 0 &&
     342          300 :                 IsUnderPostmaster &&
     343          300 :                 parse->commandType == CMD_SELECT &&
     344          124 :                 !parse->hasModifyingCTE &&
     345          124 :                 max_parallel_workers_per_gather > 0 &&
     346          124 :                 !IsParallelWorker())
     347              :         {
     348              :                 /* all the cheap tests pass, so scan the query tree */
     349          124 :                 glob->maxParallelHazard = max_parallel_hazard(parse);
     350          124 :                 glob->parallelModeOK = (glob->maxParallelHazard != PROPARALLEL_UNSAFE);
     351              :         }
     352              :         else
     353              :         {
     354              :                 /* skip the query tree scan, just assume it's unsafe */
     355          315 :                 glob->maxParallelHazard = PROPARALLEL_UNSAFE;
     356          315 :                 glob->parallelModeOK = false;
     357              :         }
     358              : 
     359              :         /*
     360              :          * glob->parallelModeNeeded is normally set to false here and changed to
     361              :          * true during plan creation if a Gather or Gather Merge plan is actually
     362              :          * created (cf. create_gather_plan, create_gather_merge_plan).
     363              :          *
     364              :          * However, if force_parallel_mode = on or force_parallel_mode = regress,
     365              :          * then we impose parallel mode whenever it's safe to do so, even if the
     366              :          * final plan doesn't use parallelism.  It's not safe to do so if the
     367              :          * query contains anything parallel-unsafe; parallelModeOK will be false
     368              :          * in that case.  Note that parallelModeOK can't change after this point.
     369              :          * Otherwise, everything in the query is either parallel-safe or
     370              :          * parallel-restricted, and in either case it should be OK to impose
     371              :          * parallel-mode restrictions.  If that ends up breaking something, then
     372              :          * either some function the user included in the query is incorrectly
     373              :          * labeled as parallel-safe or parallel-restricted when in reality it's
     374              :          * parallel-unsafe, or else the query planner itself has a bug.
     375              :          */
     376          549 :         glob->parallelModeNeeded = glob->parallelModeOK &&
     377          110 :                 (force_parallel_mode != FORCE_PARALLEL_OFF);
     378              : 
     379              :         /* Determine what fraction of the plan is likely to be scanned */
     380          439 :         if (cursorOptions & CURSOR_OPT_FAST_PLAN)
     381              :         {
     382              :                 /*
     383              :                  * We have no real idea how many tuples the user will ultimately FETCH
     384              :                  * from a cursor, but it is often the case that he doesn't want 'em
     385              :                  * all, or would prefer a fast-start plan anyway so that he can
     386              :                  * process some of the tuples sooner.  Use a GUC parameter to decide
     387              :                  * what fraction to optimize for.
     388              :                  */
     389            6 :                 tuple_fraction = cursor_tuple_fraction;
     390              : 
     391              :                 /*
     392              :                  * We document cursor_tuple_fraction as simply being a fraction, which
     393              :                  * means the edge cases 0 and 1 have to be treated specially here.  We
     394              :                  * convert 1 to 0 ("all the tuples") and 0 to a very small fraction.
     395              :                  */
     396            6 :                 if (tuple_fraction >= 1.0)
     397            0 :                         tuple_fraction = 0.0;
     398            6 :                 else if (tuple_fraction <= 0.0)
     399            0 :                         tuple_fraction = 1e-10;
     400              :         }
     401              :         else
     402              :         {
     403              :                 /* Default assumption is we need all the tuples */
     404          433 :                 tuple_fraction = 0.0;
     405              :         }
     406              : 
     407              :         /* primary planning entry point (may recurse for subqueries) */
     408          439 :         root = subquery_planner(glob, parse, NULL,
     409              :                                                         false, tuple_fraction);
     410              : 
     411              :         /* Select best Path and turn it into a Plan */
     412          439 :         final_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL);
     413          439 :         best_path = get_cheapest_fractional_path(final_rel, tuple_fraction);
     414              : 
     415          439 :         top_plan = create_plan(root, best_path);
     416              : 
     417              :         /*
     418              :          * If creating a plan for a scrollable cursor, make sure it can run
     419              :          * backwards on demand.  Add a Material node at the top at need.
     420              :          */
     421          439 :         if (cursorOptions & CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL)
     422              :         {
     423            0 :                 if (!ExecSupportsBackwardScan(top_plan))
     424            0 :                         top_plan = materialize_finished_plan(top_plan);
     425              :         }
     426              : 
     427              :         /*
     428              :          * Optionally add a Gather node for testing purposes, provided this is

     430              :          */

     433            0 :                 Gather     *gather = makeNode(Gather);
     434              : 
     435              :                 /*
     436              :                  * Top plan must not have any initPlans, else it shouldn't have been

     438              :                  */

     440              : 
     441            0 :                 gather->plan.targetlist = top_plan->targetlist;
     442            0 :                 gather->plan.qual = NIL;
     443            0 :                 gather->plan.lefttree = top_plan;
     444            0 :                 gather->plan.righttree = NULL;
     445            0 :                 gather->num_workers = 1;
     446            0 :                 gather->single_copy = true;
     447            0 :                 gather->invisible = (force_parallel_mode == FORCE_PARALLEL_REGRESS);
     448              : 
     449              :                 /*
     450              :                  * Since this Gather has no parallel-aware descendants to signal to,
     451              :                  * we don't need a rescan Param.
     452              :                  */
     453            0 :                 gather->rescan_param = -1;
     454              : 
     455              :                 /*
     456              :                  * Ideally we'd use cost_gather here, but setting up dummy path data
     457              :                  * to satisfy it doesn't seem much cleaner than knowing what it does.
     458              :                  */
     459            0 :                 gather->plan.startup_cost = top_plan->startup_cost +
     460              :                         parallel_setup_cost;
     461            0 :                 gather->plan.total_cost = top_plan->total_cost +

     463            0 :                 gather->plan.plan_rows = top_plan->plan_rows;

     473              : 
     474              :         /*

     481              :         {
     482              :                 Assert(list_length(glob->subplans) == list_length(glob->subroots));
     483          306 :                 forboth(lp, glob->subplans, lr, glob->subroots)
     484              :                 {
     485           20 :                         Plan       *subplan = (Plan *) lfirst(lp);
     486           20 :                         PlannerInfo *subroot = lfirst_node(PlannerInfo, lr);
     487              : 
     488           20 :                         SS_finalize_plan(subroot, subplan);
     489              :                 }
     490          286 :                 SS_finalize_plan(root, top_plan);
     491              :         }
     492              : 
     493              :         /* final cleanup of the plan */
     494              :         Assert(glob->finalrtable == NIL);
     495              :         Assert(glob->finalrowmarks == NIL);
     496              :         Assert(glob->resultRelations == NIL);
     497              :         Assert(glob->rootResultRelations == NIL);
     498              :         Assert(glob->appendRelations == NIL);
     499          439 :         top_plan = set_plan_references(root, top_plan);
     500              :         /* ... and the subplans (both regular subplans and initplans) */
     501              :         Assert(list_length(glob->subplans) == list_length(glob->subroots));
     502          459 :         forboth(lp, glob->subplans, lr, glob->subroots)
     503              :         {

     506              : 
     507           20 :                 lfirst(lp) = set_plan_references(subroot, subplan);
     508              :         }
     509              : 
     510              :         /* build the PlannedStmt result */

     512              :

     514          439 :         result->queryId = parse->queryId;

     517          439 :         result->canSetTag = parse->canSetTag;
     518          439 :         result->transientPlan = glob->transientPlan;

     521          439 :         result->planTree = top_plan;

     527          439 :         result->rewindPlanIDs = glob->rewindPlanIDs;
     528          439 :         result->rowMarks = glob->finalrowmarks;

     530          439 :         result->invalItems = glob->invalItems;
     531          439 :         result->paramExecTypes = glob->paramExecTypes;

     534          439 :         result->stmt_location = parse->stmt_location;
     535          439 :         result->stmt_len = parse->stmt_len;
     536              : 
     537          439 :         result->jitFlags = PGJIT_NONE;
     538          439 :         if (jit_enabled && jit_above_cost >= 0 &&
     539          439 :                 top_plan->total_cost > jit_above_cost)
     540              :         {
     541            1 :                 result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_PERFORM;
     542              : 
     543              :                 /*
     544              :                  * Decide how much effort should be put into generating better code.
     545              :                  */
     546            1 :                 if (jit_optimize_above_cost >= 0 &&
     547            1 :                         top_plan->total_cost > jit_optimize_above_cost)
     548            1 :                         result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_OPT3;
     549            1 :                 if (jit_inline_above_cost >= 0 &&
     550            1 :                         top_plan->total_cost > jit_inline_above_cost)
     551            1 :                         result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_INLINE;
     552              : 
     553              :                 /*
     554              :                  * Decide which operations should be JITed.
     555              :                  */
     556            1 :                 if (jit_expressions)
     557            1 :                         result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_EXPR;
     558            1 :                 if (jit_tuple_deforming)
     559            1 :                         result->jitFlags |= PGJIT_DEFORM;
     560              :         }
     561              : 
     562          439 :         if (glob->partition_directory != NULL)
     563           18 :                 DestroyPartitionDirectory(glob->partition_directory);
     564              : 
     565          439 :         return result;
     566              : }
     567              : 
     568              : 
     569              : /*--------------------
     570              :  * subquery_planner
     571              :  *        Invokes the planner on a subquery.  We recurse to here for each
     572              :  *        sub-SELECT found in the query tree.
     573              :  *
     574              :  * glob is the global state for the current planner run.
     575              :  * parse is the querytree produced by the parser & rewriter.
     576              :  * parent_root is the immediate parent Query's info (NULL at the top level).
     577              :  * hasRecursion is true if this is a recursive WITH query.
     578              :  * tuple_fraction is the fraction of tuples we expect will be retrieved.
     579              :  * tuple_fraction is interpreted as explained for grouping_planner, below.
     580              :  *
     581              :  * Basically, this routine does the stuff that should only be done once
     582              :  * per Query object.  It then calls grouping_planner.  At one time,
     583              :  * grouping_planner could be invoked recursively on the same Query object;
     584              :  * that's not currently true, but we keep the separation between the two
     585              :  * routines anyway, in case we need it again someday.
     586              :  *
     587              :  * subquery_planner will be called recursively to handle sub-Query nodes
     588              :  * found within the query's expressions and rangetable.
     589              :  *
     590              :  * Returns the PlannerInfo struct ("root") that contains all data generated
     591              :  * while planning the subquery.  In particular, the Path(s) attached to
     592              :  * the (UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL) upperrel represent our conclusions about the
     593              :  * cheapest way(s) to implement the query.  The top level will select the
     594              :  * best Path and pass it through createplan.c to produce a finished Plan.
     595              :  *--------------------
     596              :  */
     597              : PlannerInfo *
     598          487 : subquery_planner(PlannerGlobal *glob, Query *parse,
     599              :                                  PlannerInfo *parent_root,
     600              :                                  bool hasRecursion, double tuple_fraction)
     601              : {
     602              :         PlannerInfo *root;
     603              :         List       *newWithCheckOptions;
     604              :         List       *newHaving;
     605              :         bool            hasOuterJoins;
     606              :         bool            hasResultRTEs;
     607              :         RelOptInfo *final_rel;
     608              :         ListCell   *l;
     609              : 
     610              :         /* Create a PlannerInfo data structure for this subquery */
     611          487 :         root = makeNode(PlannerInfo);
     612          487 :         root->parse = parse;
     613          487 :         root->glob = glob;
     614          487 :         root->query_level = parent_root ? parent_root->query_level + 1 : 1;
     615          487 :         root->parent_root = parent_root;
     616          487 :         root->plan_params = NIL;
     617          487 :         root->outer_params = NULL;
     618          487 :         root->planner_cxt = CurrentMemoryContext;
     619          487 :         root->init_plans = NIL;
     620          487 :         root->cte_plan_ids = NIL;
     621          487 :         root->multiexpr_params = NIL;
     622          487 :         root->eq_classes = NIL;
     623          487 :         root->ec_merging_done = false;
     624          487 :         root->append_rel_list = NIL;
     625          487 :         root->rowMarks = NIL;
     626          487 :         memset(root->upper_rels, 0, sizeof(root->upper_rels));
     627          487 :         memset(root->upper_targets, 0, sizeof(root->upper_targets));
     628          487 :         root->processed_tlist = NIL;
     629          487 :         root->grouping_map = NULL;
     630          487 :         root->minmax_aggs = NIL;
     631          487 :         root->qual_security_level = 0;
     632          487 :         root->inhTargetKind = INHKIND_NONE;
     633          487 :         root->hasRecursion = hasRecursion;
     634          487 :         if (hasRecursion)
     635            0 :                 root->wt_param_id = assign_special_exec_param(root);
     636              :         else
     637          487 :                 root->wt_param_id = -1;
     638          487 :         root->non_recursive_path = NULL;
     639          487 :         root->partColsUpdated = false;
     640              : 
     641              :         /*
     642              :          * If there is a WITH list, process each WITH query and either convert it
     643              :          * to RTE_SUBQUERY RTE(s) or build an initplan SubPlan structure for it.
     644              :          */
     645          487 :         if (parse->cteList)
     646            4 :                 SS_process_ctes(root);
     647              : 
     648              :         /*
     649              :          * If the FROM clause is empty, replace it with a dummy RTE_RESULT RTE, so
     650              :          * that we don't need so many special cases to deal with that situation.
     651              :          */
     652          487 :         replace_empty_jointree(parse);
     653              : 
     654              :         /*
     655              :          * Look for ANY and EXISTS SubLinks in WHERE and JOIN/ON clauses, and try
     656              :          * to transform them into joins.  Note that this step does not descend
     657              :          * into subqueries; if we pull up any subqueries below, their SubLinks are
     658              :          * processed just before pulling them up.
     659              :          */
     660          487 :         if (parse->hasSubLinks)
     661           16 :                 pull_up_sublinks(root);
     662              : 
     663              :         /*
     664              :          * Scan the rangetable for function RTEs, do const-simplification on them,
     665              :          * and then inline them if possible (producing subqueries that might get
     666              :          * pulled up next).  Recursion issues here are handled in the same way as
     667              :          * for SubLinks.
     668              :          */
     669          487 :         preprocess_function_rtes(root);
     670              : 
     671              :         /*
     672              :          * Check to see if any subqueries in the jointree can be merged into this
     673              :          * query.
     674              :          */
     675          487 :         pull_up_subqueries(root);
     676              : 
     677              :         /*
     678              :          * If this is a simple UNION ALL query, flatten it into an appendrel. We
     679              :          * do this now because it requires applying pull_up_subqueries to the leaf
     680              :          * queries of the UNION ALL, which weren't touched above because they
     681              :          * weren't referenced by the jointree (they will be after we do this).
     682              :          */
     683          487 :         if (parse->setOperations)
     684            3 :                 flatten_simple_union_all(root);
     685              : 
     686              :         /*
     687              :          * Survey the rangetable to see what kinds of entries are present.  We can
     688              :          * skip some later processing if relevant SQL features are not used; for
     689              :          * example if there are no JOIN RTEs we can avoid the expense of doing
     690              :          * flatten_join_alias_vars().  This must be done after we have finished
     691              :          * adding rangetable entries, of course.  (Note: actually, processing of
     692              :          * inherited or partitioned rels can cause RTEs for their child tables to
     693              :          * get added later; but those must all be RTE_RELATION entries, so they
     694              :          * don't invalidate the conclusions drawn here.)
     695              :          */
     696          487 :         root->hasJoinRTEs = false;
     697          487 :         root->hasLateralRTEs = false;
     698          487 :         hasOuterJoins = false;
     699          487 :         hasResultRTEs = false;
     700         1396 :         foreach(l, parse->rtable)
     701              :         {
     702          909 :                 RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
     703              : 
     704          909 :                 switch (rte->rtekind)
     705              :                 {
     706          525 :                         case RTE_RELATION:
     707          525 :                                 if (rte->inh)
     708              :                                 {
     709              :                                         /*
     710              :                                          * Check to see if the relation actually has any children;
     711              :                                          * if not, clear the inh flag so we can treat it as a
     712              :                                          * plain base relation.
     713              :                                          *
     714              :                                          * Note: this could give a false-positive result, if the
     715              :                                          * rel once had children but no longer does.  We used to
     716              :                                          * be able to clear rte->inh later on when we discovered
     717              :                                          * that, but no more; we have to handle such cases as
     718              :                                          * full-fledged inheritance.
     719              :                                          */
     720          242 :                                         rte->inh = has_subclass(rte->relid);
     721              :                                 }
     722          525 :                                 break;
     723           36 :                         case RTE_JOIN:
     724           36 :                                 root->hasJoinRTEs = true;
     725           36 :                                 if (IS_OUTER_JOIN(rte->jointype))
     726           21 :                                         hasOuterJoins = true;
     727           36 :                                 break;
     728          205 :                         case RTE_RESULT:
     729          205 :                                 hasResultRTEs = true;
     730          205 :                                 break;
     731          143 :                         default:
     732              :                                 /* No work here for other RTE types */
     733          143 :                                 break;
     734              :                 }
     735              : 
     736          909 :                 if (rte->lateral)
     737            1 :                         root->hasLateralRTEs = true;
     738              : 
     739              :                 /*
     740              :                  * We can also determine the maximum security level required for any
     741              :                  * securityQuals now.  Addition of inheritance-child RTEs won't affect
     742              :                  * this, because child tables don't have their own securityQuals; see
     743              :                  * expand_single_inheritance_child().
     744              :                  */

     746            0 :                         root->qual_security_level = Max(root->qual_security_level,
     747              :                                                                                         list_length(rte->securityQuals));
     748              :         }
     749              : 
     750              :         /*
     751              :          * This would be a convenient time to check access permissions for all
     752              :          * relations mentioned in the query, since it would be better to fail now,
     753              :          * before doing any detailed planning.  However, for historical reasons,
     754              :          * we leave this to be done at executor startup.
     755              :          *
     756              :          * Note, however, that we do need to check access permissions for any view
     757              :          * relations mentioned in the query, in order to prevent information being
     758              :          * leaked by selectivity estimation functions, which only check view owner
     759              :          * permissions on underlying tables (see all_rows_selectable() and its
     760              :          * callers).  This is a little ugly, because it means that access
     761              :          * permissions for views will be checked twice, which is another reason
     762              :          * why it would be better to do all the ACL checks here.
     763              :          *
     764              :          * Similarly, if we have an inherited target relation that will be
     765              :          * processed using inheritance_planner(), we must check its access
     766              :          * permissions here.  In this case, we generate a separate plan for each
     767              :          * child target table, and the selectivity estimation functions don't see
     768              :          * the original target relation.  Therefore they only check permissions
     769              :          * (and moved-down securityQuals) on the child target tables.
     770              :          */

     772              :         {
     773          909 :                 RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);

     775              : 
     776          909 :                 if (rte->relkind == RELKIND_VIEW ||
     777          878 :                         (rti == parse->resultRelation && rte->inh))
     778              :                 {
     779              :                         bool            result;
     780              : 
     781           31 :                         result = ExecCheckRTEPerms(rte);
     782           31 :                         if (!result)
     783            0 :                                 aclcheck_error(ACLCHECK_NO_PRIV,
     784            0 :                                                            get_relkind_objtype(rte->relkind),
     785            0 :                                                            get_rel_name(rte->relid));
     786              :                 }
     787              :         }

     789              :         /*
     790              :          * Preprocess RowMark information.  We need to do this after subquery

     794              : 
     795              :         /*
     796              :          * Set hasHavingQual to remember if HAVING clause is present.  Needed

     804              : 
     805              :         /*

     946              :         /*
     947              :          * Now that we are done preprocessing expressions, and in particular done

     949              :          * They no longer match what expressions in the rest of the tree look
     950              :          * like, because we have not preprocessed expressions in those lists (and
     951              :          * do not want to; for example, expanding a SubLink there would result in
     952              :          * a useless unreferenced subplan).  Leaving them in place simply creates
     953              :          * a hazard for later scans of the tree.  We could try to prevent that by
     954              :          * using QTW_IGNORE_JOINALIASES in every tree scan done after this point,
     955              :          * but that doesn't sound very reliable.
     956              :          */

     958              :         {
     959          159 :                 foreach(l, parse->rtable)
     960              :                 {
     961          126 :                         RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, l);
     962              : 
     963          126 :                         rte->joinaliasvars = NIL;
     964              :                 }
     965              :         }
     966              : 
     967              :         /*
     968              :          * In some cases we may want to transfer a HAVING clause into WHERE. We
     969              :          * cannot do so if the HAVING clause contains aggregates (obviously) or
     970              :          * volatile functions (since a HAVING clause is supposed to be executed
     971              :          * only once per group).  We also can't do this if there are any nonempty
     972              :          * grouping sets; moving such a clause into WHERE would potentially change
     973              :          * the results, if any referenced column isn't present in all the grouping
     974              :          * sets.  (If there are only empty grouping sets, then the HAVING clause
     975              :          * must be degenerate as discussed below.)
     976              :          *
     977              :          * Also, it may be that the clause is so expensive to execute that we're
     978              :          * better off doing it only once per group, despite the loss of
     979              :          * selectivity.  This is hard to estimate short of doing the entire
     980              :          * planning process twice, so we use a heuristic: clauses containing
     981              :          * subplans are left in HAVING.  Otherwise, we move or copy the HAVING
     982              :          * clause into WHERE, in hopes of eliminating tuples before aggregation
     983              :          * instead of after.
     984              :          *
     985              :          * If the query has explicit grouping then we can simply move such a
     986              :          * clause into WHERE; any group that fails the clause will not be in the
     987              :          * output because none of its tuples will reach the grouping or
     988              :          * aggregation stage.  Otherwise we must have a degenerate (variable-free)
     989              :          * HAVING clause, which we put in WHERE so that query_planner() can use it
     990              :          * in a gating Result node, but also keep in HAVING to ensure that we
     991              :          * don't emit a bogus aggregated row. (This could be done better, but it
     992              :          * seems not worth optimizing.)
     993              :          *
     994              :          * Note that both havingQual and parse->jointree->quals are in
     995              :          * implicitly-ANDed-list form at this point, even though they are declared
     996              :          * as Node *.
     997              :          */
     998          487 :         newHaving = NIL;
     999          488 :         foreach(l, (List *) parse->havingQual)
    1000              :         {
    1001            1 :                 Node       *havingclause = (Node *) lfirst(l);
    1002              : 
    1003            2 :                 if ((parse->groupClause && parse->groupingSets) ||
    1004            1 :                         contain_agg_clause(havingclause) ||
    1005            0 :                         contain_volatile_functions(havingclause) ||
    1006            0 :                         contain_subplans(havingclause))
    1007              :                 {
    1008              :                         /* keep it in HAVING */
    1009            1 :                         newHaving = lappend(newHaving, havingclause);
    1010              :                 }
    1011            0 :                 else if (parse->groupClause && !parse->groupingSets)
    1012              :                 {
    1013              :                         /* move it to WHERE */
    1014            0 :                         parse->jointree->quals = (Node *)
    1015            0 :                                 lappend((List *) parse->jointree->quals, havingclause);
    1016              :                 }
    1017              :                 else
    1018              :                 {
    1019              :                         /* put a copy in WHERE, keep it in HAVING */
    1020            0 :                         parse->jointree->quals = (Node *)
    1021            0 :                                 lappend((List *) parse->jointree->quals,
    1022              :                                                 copyObject(havingclause));
    1023            0 :                         newHaving = lappend(newHaving, havingclause);
    1024              :                 }
    1025              :         }
    1026          487 :         parse->havingQual = (Node *) newHaving;
    1027              : 
    1028              :         /* Remove any redundant GROUP BY columns */
    1029          487 :         remove_useless_groupby_columns(root);
    1030              : 
    1031              :         /*
    1032              :          * If we have any outer joins, try to reduce them to plain inner joins.
    1033              :          * This step is most easily done after we've done expression
    1034              :          * preprocessing.
    1035              :          */
    1036          487 :         if (hasOuterJoins)
    1037           20 :                 reduce_outer_joins(root);
    1038              : 
    1039              :         /*
    1040              :          * If we have any RTE_RESULT relations, see if they can be deleted from
    1041              :          * the jointree.  This step is most effectively done after we've done
    1042              :          * expression preprocessing and outer join reduction.
    1043              :          */
    1044          487 :         if (hasResultRTEs)
    1045          205 :                 remove_useless_result_rtes(root);
    1046              : 
    1047              :         /*
    1048              :          * Do the main planning.  If we have an inherited target relation, that
    1049              :          * needs special processing, else go straight to grouping_planner.
    1050              :          */
    1051          487 :         if (parse->resultRelation &&
    1052          261 :                 rt_fetch(parse->resultRelation, parse->rtable)->inh)
    1053            0 :                 inheritance_planner(root);
    1054              :         else
    1055          487 :                 grouping_planner(root, false, tuple_fraction);
    1056              : 
    1057              :         /*
    1058              :          * Capture the set of outer-level param IDs we have access to, for use in
    1059              :          * extParam/allParam calculations later.
    1060              :          */
    1061          487 :         SS_identify_outer_params(root);
    1062              : 
    1063              :         /*
    1064              :          * If any initPlans were created in this query level, adjust the surviving

    1067              :          * create_plan() runs, but we must include their effects now.
    1068              :          */
    1069          487 :         final_rel = fetch_upper_rel(root, UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL);
    1070          487 :         SS_charge_for_initplans(root, final_rel);
    1071              : 
    1072              :         /*
    1073              :          * Make sure we've identified the cheapest Path for the final rel.  (By
    1074              :          * doing this here not in grouping_planner, we include initPlan costs in
    1075              :          * the decision, though it's unlikely that will change anything.)
    1076              :          */
    1077          487 :         set_cheapest(final_rel);
    1078              : 
    1079          487 :         return root;
    1080              : }
    1081              : 
    1082              : /*
    1083              :  * preprocess_expression
    1084              :  *              Do subquery_planner's preprocessing work for an expression,
    1085              :  *              which can be a targetlist, a WHERE clause (including JOIN/ON
    1086              :  *              conditions), a HAVING clause, or a few other things.
    1087              :  */
    1088              : static Node *
    1089         3515 : preprocess_expression(PlannerInfo *root, Node *expr, int kind)
    1090              : {
    1091              :         /*
    1092              :          * Fall out quickly if expression is empty.  This occurs often enough to
    1093              :          * be worth checking.  Note that null->null is the correct conversion for
    1094              :          * implicit-AND result format, too.
    1095              :          */
    1096         3515 :         if (expr == NULL)
    1097         2798 :                 return NULL;
    1098              : 
    1099              :         /*
    1100              :          * If the query has any join RTEs, replace join alias variables with
    1101              :          * base-relation variables.  We must do this first, since any expressions
    1102              :          * we may extract from the joinaliasvars lists have not been preprocessed.
    1103              :          * For example, if we did this after sublink processing, sublinks expanded
    1104              :          * out from join aliases would not get processed.  But we can skip this in
    1105              :          * non-lateral RTE functions, VALUES lists, and TABLESAMPLE clauses, since
    1106              :          * they can't contain any Vars of the current query level.
    1107              :          */
    1108          717 :         if (root->hasJoinRTEs &&
    1109          196 :                 !(kind == EXPRKIND_RTFUNC ||
    1110           98 :                   kind == EXPRKIND_VALUES ||
    1111              :                   kind == EXPRKIND_TABLESAMPLE ||
    1112              :                   kind == EXPRKIND_TABLEFUNC))
    1113           98 :                 expr = flatten_join_alias_vars(root->parse, expr);
    1114              : 
    1115              :         /*
    1116              :          * Simplify constant expressions.  For function RTEs, this was already
    1117              :          * done by preprocess_function_rtes.  (But note we must do it again for
    1118              :          * EXPRKIND_RTFUNC_LATERAL, because those might by now contain
    1119              :          * un-simplified subexpressions inserted by flattening of subqueries or
    1120              :          * join alias variables.)
    1121              :          *
    1122              :          * Note: an essential effect of this is to convert named-argument function
    1123              :          * calls to positional notation and insert the current actual values of
    1124              :          * any default arguments for functions.  To ensure that happens, we *must*
    1125              :          * process all expressions here.  Previous PG versions sometimes skipped
    1126              :          * const-simplification if it didn't seem worth the trouble, but we can't
    1127              :          * do that anymore.
    1128              :          *
    1129              :          * Note: this also flattens nested AND and OR expressions into N-argument
    1130              :          * form.  All processing of a qual expression after this point must be
    1131              :          * careful to maintain AND/OR flatness --- that is, do not generate a tree
    1132              :          * with AND directly under AND, nor OR directly under OR.
    1133              :          */
    1134          717 :         if (kind != EXPRKIND_RTFUNC)
    1135          710 :                 expr = eval_const_expressions(root, expr);
    1136              : 
    1137              :         /*
    1138              :          * If it's a qual or havingQual, canonicalize it.
    1139              :          */
    1140          717 :         if (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL)
    1141              :         {
    1142          193 :                 expr = (Node *) canonicalize_qual((Expr *) expr, false);
    1143              : 
    1144              : #ifdef OPTIMIZER_DEBUG
    1145              :                 printf("After canonicalize_qual()\n");
    1146              :                 pprint(expr);
    1147              : #endif
    1148              :         }
    1149              : 
    1150              :         /* Expand SubLinks to SubPlans */
    1151          717 :         if (root->parse->hasSubLinks)
    1152           57 :                 expr = SS_process_sublinks(root, expr, (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL));
    1153              : 
    1154              :         /*
    1155              :          * XXX do not insert anything here unless you have grokked the comments in
    1156              :          * SS_replace_correlation_vars ...
    1157              :          */
    1158              : 
    1159              :         /* Replace uplevel vars with Param nodes (this IS possible in VALUES) */
    1160          717 :         if (root->query_level > 1)
    1161           67 :                 expr = SS_replace_correlation_vars(root, expr);
    1162              : 
    1163              :         /*
    1164              :          * If it's a qual or havingQual, convert it to implicit-AND format. (We
    1165              :          * don't want to do this before eval_const_expressions, since the latter
    1166              :          * would be unable to simplify a top-level AND correctly. Also,
    1167              :          * SS_process_sublinks expects explicit-AND format.)
    1168              :          */
    1169          717 :         if (kind == EXPRKIND_QUAL)
    1170          193 :                 expr = (Node *) make_ands_implicit((Expr *) expr);
    1171              : 
    1172          717 :         return expr;
    1173              : }
    1174              : 
    1175              : /*
    1176              :  * preprocess_qual_conditions
    1177              :  *              Recursively scan the query's jointree and do subquery_planner's

    1181         1086 : preprocess_qual_conditions(PlannerInfo *root, Node *jtnode)
    1182              : {

    1184            0 :                 return;
    1185         1086 :         if (IsA(jtnode, RangeTblRef))
    1186              :         {
    1187              :                 /* nothing to do here */
    1188              :         }
    1189          549 :         else if (IsA(jtnode, FromExpr))
    1190              :         {
    1191          506 :                 FromExpr   *f = (FromExpr *) jtnode;
    1192              :                 ListCell   *l;
    1193              : 
    1194         1019 :                 foreach(l, f->fromlist)
    1195          513 :                         preprocess_qual_conditions(root, lfirst(l));

    1197          506 :                 f->quals = preprocess_expression(root, f->quals, EXPRKIND_QUAL);
    1198              :         }
    1199           43 :         else if (IsA(jtnode, JoinExpr))
    1200              :         {
    1201           43 :                 JoinExpr   *j = (JoinExpr *) jtnode;
    1202              : 
    1203           43 :                 preprocess_qual_conditions(root, j->larg);

    1206           43 :                 j->quals = preprocess_expression(root, j->quals, EXPRKIND_QUAL);

    1210              :                          (int) nodeTag(jtnode));

    1213              : /*
    1214              :  * preprocess_phv_expression
    1215              :  *        Do preprocessing on a PlaceHolderVar expression that's been pulled up.
    1216              :  *
    1217              :  * If a LATERAL subquery references an output of another subquery, and that
    1218              :  * output must be wrapped in a PlaceHolderVar because of an intermediate outer
    1219              :  * join, then we'll push the PlaceHolderVar expression down into the subquery
    1220              :  * and later pull it back up during find_lateral_references, which runs after
    1221              :  * subquery_planner has preprocessed all the expressions that were in the

    1225            0 : preprocess_phv_expression(PlannerInfo *root, Expr *expr)

    1230              : /*
    1231              :  * inheritance_planner
    1232              :  *        Generate Paths in the case where the result relation is an
    1233              :  *        inheritance set.
    1234              :  *
    1235              :  * We have to handle this case differently from cases where a source relation
    1236              :  * is an inheritance set. Source inheritance is expanded at the bottom of the
    1237              :  * plan tree (see allpaths.c), but target inheritance has to be expanded at
    1238              :  * the top.  The reason is that for UPDATE, each target relation needs a
    1239              :  * different targetlist matching its own column set.  Fortunately,
    1240              :  * the UPDATE/DELETE target can never be the nullable side of an outer join,
    1241              :  * so it's OK to generate the plan this way.
    1242              :  *
    1243              :  * Returns nothing; the useful output is in the Paths we attach to
    1244              :  * the (UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL) upperrel stored in *root.
    1245              :  *
    1246              :  * Note that we have not done set_cheapest() on the final rel; it's convenient
    1247              :  * to leave this to the caller.
    1248              :  */
    1249              : static void
    1250            0 : inheritance_planner(PlannerInfo *root)
    1251              : {
    1252            0 :         Query      *parse = root->parse;
    1253            0 :         int                     top_parentRTindex = parse->resultRelation;
    1254              :         List       *select_rtable;
    1255              :         List       *select_appinfos;
    1256              :         List       *child_appinfos;
    1257              :         List       *old_child_rtis;
    1258              :         List       *new_child_rtis;
    1259              :         Bitmapset  *subqueryRTindexes;
    1260              :         Index           next_subquery_rti;
    1261            0 :         int                     nominalRelation = -1;
    1262            0 :         Index           rootRelation = 0;
    1263            0 :         List       *final_rtable = NIL;
    1264            0 :         List       *final_rowmarks = NIL;
    1265            0 :         List       *final_appendrels = NIL;
    1266            0 :         int                     save_rel_array_size = 0;
    1267            0 :         RelOptInfo **save_rel_array = NULL;
    1268            0 :         AppendRelInfo **save_append_rel_array = NULL;
    1269            0 :         List       *subpaths = NIL;
    1270            0 :         List       *subroots = NIL;
    1271            0 :         List       *resultRelations = NIL;
    1272            0 :         List       *withCheckOptionLists = NIL;
    1273            0 :         List       *returningLists = NIL;
    1274              :         List       *rowMarks;
    1275              :         RelOptInfo *final_rel;
    1276              :         ListCell   *lc;
    1277              :         ListCell   *lc2;
    1278              :         Index           rti;
    1279              :         RangeTblEntry *parent_rte;
    1280              :         Bitmapset  *parent_relids;
    1281              :         Query     **parent_parses;
    1282              : 
    1283              :         /* Should only get here for UPDATE or DELETE */

    1287              :         /*
    1288              :          * We generate a modified instance of the original Query for each target
    1289              :          * relation, plan that, and put all the plans into a list that will be
    1290              :          * controlled by a single ModifyTable node.  All the instances share the
    1291              :          * same rangetable, but each instance must have its own set of subquery
    1292              :          * RTEs within the finished rangetable because (1) they are likely to get
    1293              :          * scribbled on during planning, and (2) it's not inconceivable that
    1294              :          * subqueries could get planned differently in different cases.  We need
    1295              :          * not create duplicate copies of other RTE kinds, in particular not the
    1296              :          * target relations, because they don't have either of those issues.  Not
    1297              :          * having to duplicate the target relations is important because doing so
    1298              :          * (1) would result in a rangetable of length O(N^2) for N targets, with
    1299              :          * at least O(N^3) work expended here; and (2) would greatly complicate
    1300              :          * management of the rowMarks list.
    1301              :          *
    1302              :          * To begin with, generate a bitmapset of the relids of the subquery RTEs.
    1303              :          */

    1305            0 :         rti = 1;
    1306            0 :         foreach(lc, parse->rtable)
    1307              :         {
    1308            0 :                 RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, lc);
    1309              : 
    1310            0 :                 if (rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY)
    1311            0 :                         subqueryRTindexes = bms_add_member(subqueryRTindexes, rti);
    1312            0 :                 rti++;
    1313              :         }
    1314              : 
    1315              :         /*

    1323              :         Assert(parent_rte->inh);

    1325              :         {
    1326            0 :                 nominalRelation = top_parentRTindex;

    1329              : 
    1330              :         /*
    1331              :          * Before generating the real per-child-relation plans, do a cycle of
    1332              :          * planning as though the query were a SELECT.  The objective here is to

    1334              :          * expansion and pruning logic as for a SELECT.  We'll then pull out the
    1335              :          * RangeTblEntry-s generated for the child rels, and make use of the
    1336              :          * AppendRelInfo entries for them to guide the real planning.  (This is
    1337              :          * rather inefficient; we could perhaps stop short of making a full Path

    1341              :         {
    1342              :                 PlannerInfo *subroot;
    1343              : 
    1344              :                 /*
    1345              :                  * Flat-copy the PlannerInfo to prevent modification of the original.
    1346              :                  */
    1347            0 :                 subroot = makeNode(PlannerInfo);
    1348            0 :                 memcpy(subroot, root, sizeof(PlannerInfo));
    1349              : 
    1350              :                 /*

    1354              :                  * expand_partitioned_rtentry will correctly update

    1356              :                  */

    1358              : 
    1359            0 :                 subroot->parse->commandType = CMD_SELECT;
    1360            0 :                 subroot->parse->resultRelation = 0;
    1361              : 
    1362              :                 /*

    1369              : 
    1370              :                 /*

    1387              :                 /*

    1393              :         }
    1394              : 
    1395              :         /*----------
    1396              :          * Since only one rangetable can exist in the final plan, we need to make
    1397              :          * sure that it contains all the RTEs needed for any child plan.  This is
    1398              :          * complicated by the need to use separate subquery RTEs for each child.
    1399              :          * We arrange the final rtable as follows:
    1400              :          * 1. All original rtable entries (with their original RT indexes).
    1401              :          * 2. All the relation RTEs generated for children of the target table.
    1402              :          * 3. Subquery RTEs for children after the first.  We need N * (K - 1)
    1403              :          *    RT slots for this, if there are N subqueries and K child tables.
    1404              :          * 4. Additional RTEs generated during the child planning runs, such as
    1405              :          *    children of inheritable RTEs other than the target table.
    1406              :          * We assume that each child planning run will create an identical set
    1407              :          * of type-4 RTEs.
    1408              :          *
    1409              :          * So the next thing to do is append the type-2 RTEs (the target table's
    1410              :          * children) to the original rtable.  We look through select_appinfos
    1411              :          * to find them.
    1412              :          *
    1413              :          * To identify which AppendRelInfos are relevant as we thumb through
    1414              :          * select_appinfos, we need to look for both direct and indirect children
    1415              :          * of top_parentRTindex, so we use a bitmap of known parent relids.
    1416              :          * expand_inherited_rtentry() always processes a parent before any of that
    1417              :          * parent's children, so we should see an intermediate parent before its
    1418              :          * children.
    1419              :          *----------
    1420              :          */

    1422            0 :         old_child_rtis = NIL;
    1423            0 :         new_child_rtis = NIL;
    1424            0 :         parent_relids = bms_make_singleton(top_parentRTindex);
    1425            0 :         foreach(lc, select_appinfos)
    1426              :         {
    1427            0 :                 AppendRelInfo *appinfo = lfirst_node(AppendRelInfo, lc);
    1428              :                 RangeTblEntry *child_rte;
    1429              : 
    1430              :                 /* append_rel_list contains all append rels; ignore others */
    1431            0 :                 if (!bms_is_member(appinfo->parent_relid, parent_relids))
    1432            0 :                         continue;
    1433              : 
    1434              :                 /* remember relevant AppendRelInfos for use below */
    1435            0 :                 child_appinfos = lappend(child_appinfos, appinfo);
    1436              : 
    1437              :                 /* extract RTE for this child rel */

    1442              :

    1445              :

    1450            0 :                 if (child_rte->inh)
    1451              :                 {
    1452              :                         Assert(child_rte->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE);
    1453            0 :                         parent_relids = bms_add_member(parent_relids, appinfo->child_relid);
    1454              :                 }
    1455              :         }
    1456              : 
    1457              :         /*

    1459              :          * final rtable are different from what they were in the SELECT query.
    1460              :          * Adjust the AppendRelInfos so that they will correctly map RT indexes to
    1461              :          * the final indexes.  We can do this left-to-right since no child rel's
    1462              :          * final RT index could be greater than what it had in the SELECT query.

    1468              : 
    1469            0 :                 if (old_child_rti == new_child_rti)
    1470            0 :                         continue;                       /* nothing to do */
    1471              : 
    1472              :                 Assert(old_child_rti > new_child_rti);
    1473              : 
    1474            0 :                 ChangeVarNodes((Node *) child_appinfos,
    1475              :                                            old_child_rti, new_child_rti, 0);
    1476              :         }
    1477              : 
    1478              :         /*

    1488              : 
    1489            0 :                 while (n_children-- > 1)
    1490              :                 {
    1491            0 :                         int                     oldrti = -1;
    1492              : 
    1493            0 :                         while ((oldrti = bms_next_member(subqueryRTindexes, oldrti)) >= 0)

    1498            0 :                                 parse->rtable = lappend(parse->rtable, copyObject(subqrte));
    1499              :                         }
    1500              :                 }
    1501              :         }
    1502              : 
    1503              :         /*
    1504              :          * The query for each child is obtained by translating the query for its

    1507              :          * remember the appropriate query trees.  This is indexed by parent relid.
    1508              :          * Since the maximum number of parents is limited by the number of RTEs in
    1509              :          * the SELECT query, we use that number to allocate the array.  An extra

    1529              :                 /*

    1571              : 
    1572              :                 /*

    1576              :                  * however, need to remember its modified PlannerInfo for use when
    1577              :                  * processing its children, since we'll update their varnos based on

    1592              :                 /*

    1596              :                  * non-partitioned inheritance cases, we'll use the first child
    1597              :                  * relation (even if it's excluded) as the nominal target relation.
    1598              :                  * Because of the way expand_inherited_rtentry works, that should be
    1599              :                  * the RTE representing the parent table in its role as a simple
    1600              :                  * member of the inheritance set.
    1601              :                  *

    1603              :                  * parent RTE as the nominal relation; but that RTE is not otherwise

    1844              : 
    1845              : /*--------------------
    1846              :  * grouping_planner
    1847              :  *        Perform planning steps related to grouping, aggregation, etc.
    1848              :  *
    1849              :  * This function adds all required top-level processing to the scan/join
    1850              :  * Path(s) produced by query_planner.
    1851              :  *
    1852              :  * If inheritance_update is true, we're being called from inheritance_planner
    1853              :  * and should not include a ModifyTable step in the resulting Path(s).
    1854              :  * (inheritance_planner will create a single ModifyTable node covering all the
    1855              :  * target tables.)
    1856              :  *
    1857              :  * tuple_fraction is the fraction of tuples we expect will be retrieved.
    1858              :  * tuple_fraction is interpreted as follows:
    1859              :  *        0: expect all tuples to be retrieved (normal case)
    1860              :  *        0 < tuple_fraction < 1: expect the given fraction of tuples available
    1861              :  *              from the plan to be retrieved
    1862              :  *        tuple_fraction >= 1: tuple_fraction is the absolute number of tuples
    1863              :  *              expected to be retrieved (ie, a LIMIT specification)
    1864              :  *
    1865              :  * Returns nothing; the useful output is in the Paths we attach to the
    1866              :  * (UPPERREL_FINAL, NULL) upperrel in *root.  In addition,
    1867              :  * root->processed_tlist contains the final processed targetlist.
    1868              :  *
    1869              :  * Note that we have not done set_cheapest() on the final rel; it's convenient
    1870              :  * to leave this to the caller.
    1871              :  *--------------------
    1872              :  */
    1873              : static void
    1874          487 : grouping_planner(PlannerInfo *root, bool inheritance_update,
    1875              :                                  double tuple_fraction)
    1876              : {
    1877          487 :         Query      *parse = root->parse;
    1878          487 :         int64           offset_est = 0;
    1879          487 :         int64           count_est = 0;
    1880          487 :         double          limit_tuples = -1.0;
    1881          487 :         bool            have_postponed_srfs = false;
    1882              :         PathTarget *final_target;
    1883              :         List       *final_targets;
    1884              :         List       *final_targets_contain_srfs;
    1885              :         bool            final_target_parallel_safe;
    1886              :         RelOptInfo *current_rel;
    1887              :         RelOptInfo *final_rel;
    1888              :         FinalPathExtraData extra;
    1889              :         ListCell   *lc;
    1890              :

    2655              :                                                  List *gsets,

    2798              : /*
    2799              :  * Select RowMarkType to use for a given table
    2800              :  */
    2801              : RowMarkType
    2802           30 : select_rowmark_type(RangeTblEntry *rte, LockClauseStrength strength)
    2803              : {
    2804           30 :         if (rte->rtekind != RTE_RELATION)
    2805              :         {
    2806              :                 /* If it's not a table at all, use ROW_MARK_COPY */

    2808              :         }
    2809           28 :         else if (rte->relkind == RELKIND_FOREIGN_TABLE)
    2810              :         {

    2814            0 :                 if (fdwroutine->GetForeignRowMarkType != NULL)
    2815            0 :                         return fdwroutine->GetForeignRowMarkType(rte, strength);
    2816              :                 /* Otherwise, use ROW_MARK_COPY by default */
    2817            0 :                 return ROW_MARK_COPY;
    2818              :         }
    2819              :         else
    2820              :         {
    2821              :                 /* Regular table, apply the appropriate lock type */
    2822           28 :                 switch (strength)
    2823              :                 {
    2824            3 :                         case LCS_NONE:
    2825              : 
    2826              :                                 /*
    2827              :                                  * We don't need a tuple lock, only the ability to re-fetch
    2828              :                                  * the row.
    2829              :                                  */
    2830            3 :                                 return ROW_MARK_REFERENCE;
    2831              :                                 break;
    2832           18 :                         case LCS_FORKEYSHARE:
    2833           18 :                                 return ROW_MARK_KEYSHARE;
    2834              :                                 break;
    2835            1 :                         case LCS_FORSHARE:
    2836            1 :                                 return ROW_MARK_SHARE;
    2837              :                                 break;
    2838            0 :                         case LCS_FORNOKEYUPDATE:
    2839            0 :                                 return ROW_MARK_NOKEYEXCLUSIVE;
    2840              :                                 break;
    2841            6 :                         case LCS_FORUPDATE:
    2842            6 :                                 return ROW_MARK_EXCLUSIVE;
    2843              :                                 break;
    2844              :                 }
    2845            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "unrecognized LockClauseStrength %d", (int) strength);
    2846              :                 return ROW_MARK_EXCLUSIVE;      /* keep compiler quiet */
    2847              :         }
    2848              : }
    2849              : 
    2850              : /*
    2851              :  * preprocess_limit - do pre-estimation for LIMIT and/or OFFSET clauses
    2852              :  *
    2853              :  * We try to estimate the values of the LIMIT/OFFSET clauses, and pass the
    2854              :  * results back in *count_est and *offset_est.  These variables are set to
    2855              :  * 0 if the corresponding clause is not present, and -1 if it's present
    2856              :  * but we couldn't estimate the value for it.  (The "0" convention is OK
    2857              :  * for OFFSET but a little bit bogus for LIMIT: effectively we estimate
    2858              :  * LIMIT 0 as though it were LIMIT 1.  But this is in line with the planner's
    2859              :  * usual practice of never estimating less than one row.)  These values will
    2860              :  * be passed to create_limit_path, which see if you change this code.
    2861              :  *

    3092              : /*
    3093              :  * remove_useless_groupby_columns
    3094              :  *              Remove any columns in the GROUP BY clause that are redundant due to
    3095              :  *              being functionally dependent on other GROUP BY columns.
    3096              :  *
    3097              :  * Since some other DBMSes do not allow references to ungrouped columns, it's
    3098              :  * not unusual to find all columns listed in GROUP BY even though listing the
    3099              :  * primary-key columns would be sufficient.  Deleting such excess columns
    3100              :  * avoids redundant sorting work, so it's worth doing.
    3101              :  *
    3102              :  * Relcache invalidations will ensure that cached plans become invalidated
    3103              :  * when the underlying index of the pkey constraint is dropped.
    3104              :  *
    3105              :  * Currently, we only make use of pkey constraints for this, however, we may
    3106              :  * wish to take this further in the future and also use unique constraints
    3107              :  * which have NOT NULL columns.  In that case, plan invalidation will still
    3108              :  * work since relations will receive a relcache invalidation when a NOT NULL
    3109              :  * constraint is dropped.
    3110              :  */

    3230            0 :                 foreach(lc, parse->groupClause)
